results.rendered_html: sam-pheṭa m. (prob. for saṃ-spheṭa, q.v.) mutual conflict or encounter of angry persons, angry or passionate contention (in dram. one of the 13 elements of the vimarśa [q.v.], illustrated by the altercation between Śakuntalā and the king in  Śak. ; or as one of the 4 elements of the ārabhaṭi [q.v.], illustrated by the encounter of Mādhava and Aghora-ghaṇṭa in  Mālatīm. ),  Bhar. ;  Daśar. ;  Sāh.
